Growers have argued that the current price realization is far below production costs and have sought a range of ₹8,000 to ₹10,000 per tonne instead of the existing ₹2,000 to ₹3,000. The issue is expected to be taken up in consultation with the agriculture department and local cooperative bodies. For the Union Territory, the move is politically significant because mango cultivation remains important in rural belts and any price intervention could influence farmer sentiment ahead of the next budget cycle.
